tools.name: one_shot
tools.description:
End-to-end deploy: generate strategy → train → deploy live.

    One of `prompt` (free-form NL), `preset` (curated winning strategy), or
    `community_id` (copy a published community strategy) is required. If more
    than one is passed, precedence is community_id > preset > prompt.

    Args:

tools.name: get_deploy_result
tools.description:
Wait for a `one_shot` deploy to finish and return its final result.

    `one_shot` returns a job_token immediately and the LIVE CARD already streams
    progress and renders the interactive backtest chart itself. Call this ONCE with
    the token to get the final numbers as TEXT so you can summariz

tools.name: list_models
tools.description: List the user's trained models with pre-computed train/test stats.

tools.name: list_deployed
tools.description: List the user's currently deployed (live) models.

tools.name: generate_strategy
tools.description:
Generate Python strategy code (no training/deploy). Use when the user wants raw code.

    Args:
      features: NL description of features (e.g. "RSI 14, Bollinger Bands").
      signals: NL description of signal logic (e.g. "Buy when RSI < 30").
      model: ML model name (default Random Forest).

tools.name: browse_community
tools.description:
Browse the public community leaderboard of published strategies, ranked
    by a composite performance score (best first). No signup or key needed.

    Copy-trade flow: call this to find a top strategy, then pass its `id` to
    `one_shot` as `community_id` to deploy a live signal model running tha

tools.name: find_strategy
tools.description:
Find an existing PROVEN strategy that matches a plain-English idea, so you
    can offer the user a choice — deploy the existing one, or generate a fresh
    custom one. Mirrors the quantifyme.ai landing experience: "Found <X> by
    @<author> (WR/PF) — Use it / Generate fresh".

    CALL THIS FIRST

tools.name: get_strategy_code
tools.description:
Get the actual Python code behind a community leaderboard strategy.

    Use after `browse_community`: pass an entry's `id` here to read its real
    `feature_engineering()` + `strategy_config()` source so the user can
    inspect or tweak it. To deploy it unchanged, pass the same id to
    `one_sho

tools.name: get_quote
tools.description:
Get the latest price for a G7 FX pair — a quick "what's it at now" check.

    Useful for context before deploying a strategy. The price is the close of
    the most recent 1-minute bar from the platform's market feed (not a raw
    live tick); FX markets close on weekends, so the `stale` flag marks

tools.name: get_model_chart
tools.description:
Visualize a trained model's backtest — a cumulative-return chart + trade log + stats.

    Use after `one_shot` / `list_models` with the model's `stem` to SHOW the user how it
    traded (the "is it actually any good" view). In ChatGPT this renders an interactive
    widget. In Claude, render an int

tools.name: stream_test
tools.description:
Diagnostic: test whether LIVE data streaming works in this client.

    Renders a widget with three panels — a JS timer (baseline), a WebSocket to
    the live price feed, and an HTTP poll of /quote — each showing a live value
    + status, so you can see exactly which streaming mechanisms the clien

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "one_shot" stacks urgency/override words (immediately, always, ignore). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: End-to-end deploy: generate strategy → train → deploy live. One of `prompt` (free-form NL), `preset` (curated winning strategy), or `community_id` (copy a publi…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "get_deploy_result" stacks urgency/override words (important, immediately). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Wait for a `one_shot` deploy to finish and return its final result. `one_shot` returns a job_token immediately and the LIVE CARD already streams progress and re…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: one_shot, get_quote · Private data: get_deploy_result · Public sink: one_shot, get_deploy_result.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: one_shot, get_quote · Private data: get_deploy_result · Public sink: one_shot, get_deploy_result

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
